_comment: Amount parsing conventions discovered in Mexican bank statement PDFs. Each convention describes how to determine the sign of an amount.

conventions.description: Bare positive number = charge (negative). Number followed by 'CR' on same or next line = credit (positive).
conventions.detect_hint: Look for isolated 'CR' lines or 'CR' suffix after amounts
conventions.sourced_from: Amex Mexico 201901.pdf — charges are bare numbers, credits have 'CR' suffix

conventions.description: Separate deposit (positive) and withdrawal (negative) columns. Amount is always positive in its column; sign determined by which column it's in.
conventions.detect_hint: Two amount columns identified by headers like 'Depósito/Retiro' or 'Abono/Cargo'
conventions.sourced_from: Openbank Mexico 202508.pdf — 'Depósito Retiro Saldo' three-column amount layout

conventions.description: Negative numbers have explicit minus sign. Positive numbers are credits.
conventions.detect_hint: Minus sign visible before some amounts
conventions.sourced_from: Generic convention — not yet observed in current samples

conventions.description: Amounts wrapped in parentheses are negative (charges). Plain amounts are credits.
conventions.sourced_from: Generic convention — not yet observed in current samples
